fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Idaho Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Idaho - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity idaho, how to transfer dietitian license to idaho, dietitian compact idaho, idaho dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Dietetic Licensure Board
board.parent_dept: Idaho Division of Occupational and Professional Licenses (DOPL)

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (transitions from annual $60 to biennial $120 starting mid-2025)

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The credential to practice dietetics in Idaho, regulated by the Idaho Dietetic Licensure Board under DOPL's Board of Medicine.

license_types.title: Provisional Dietitian
license_types.description: Available for applicants who have completed their education and supervised practice but have not yet passed the CDR exam. Valid for one year, costs $50, and allows practice under the supervision of a Licensed Dietitian.

steps.title: Fulfill Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you meet Idaho's requirements, which include holding a bachelor's or master's degree in a relevant field, completing a supervised practice experience approved by ACEND, and passing the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) examination.

steps.title: Create an Access Idaho Account
steps.description: Navigate to the Idaho Board of Medicine's "Access Idaho" online portal at https://accessidaho.idaho.gov/apps/bom/ and register for a new user account if you do not already have one.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your "Access Idaho" account, select the option to 'Apply for a License,' and accurately complete all sections of the Licensed Dietitian application.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts, verification of supervised practice completion, and official CDR exam scores to be sent directly to the Idaho Board of Medicine from the respective institutions or organizations.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable initial application fee of $100 through the "Access Idaho" online portal using a valid payment method.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Licensure
steps.description: The Board of Medicine will review your complete application and all supporting documents. Once approved, your Licensed Dietitian (LD) license will be issued.

documents.detail: Application form with your notarized signature (if using paper form).
Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree (if not using CDR as sole verification).

provisional_text: Idaho offers a Provisional License for $50 for individuals who have fulfilled the educational and supervised practice requirements but have not yet passed the CDR exam. A Provisional Licensed Dietitian must practice under the direction and supervision of an Idaho Licensed Dietitian. The provisional license is valid for one year.
reciprocity_text: Idaho allows for licensure by endorsement. Out-of-state applicants with a current, valid license from a state with equivalent standards or those holding active CDR registration can apply by submitting the standard application, fee, and proof of out-of-state licensure.

faqs.question: How much does an Idaho dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial license application fee is $100. A provisional license costs $50.

faqs.question: When does the Idaho license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Starting in mid-2025, Idaho dietitian licenses transition to a biennial (two-year) renewal cycle based on the licensee's birth year. The biennial renewal fee is $120.

faqs.question: Is the Dietitian Licensure Compact active in Idaho?
faqs.answer: As of early 2026, Idaho has pending legislation (S1284) to adopt the Dietitian Licensure Compact.

faqs.question: Does Idaho have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Idaho. To work in Idaho, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Idaho?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 3 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Idaho while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Idaho allows for licensure by endorsement. Out-of-state applicants with a current, valid license from a state with equivalent standards or those holding active CDR registration can apply by submitting the standard application, fee, and proof of out-of-state licensure.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Idaho
hero.dek: To apply for a Licensed Dietitian (LD) license in Idaho, new applicants must use the Idaho Board of Medicine's "Access Idaho" online portal to submit their application and required documentation.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the Idaho Board of Medicine's online portal, click 'New User Registration' or 'Apply for a License,' and follow the prompts to create an account with a unique username and password to begin your application.
